title: rubus foliosus subsp. saltuum
intro: Rubus foliosus subsp. saltuum, also known as the saltbush blackberry, is a subspecies of blackberry native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its leaves and roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rubus foliosus subsp. saltuum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
